---
title: "Interactive Quarto Tutorial"
---

# Build your first Quarto website

Welcome to the interactive tutorial. Each section below helps you build, customize, and publish a Quarto website page-by-page.

::: callout-important
This tutorial is designed for beginners. You do not need R to follow along — plain Markdown and Quarto are enough.
:::

## 1. Start with a page

Every Quarto page begins with a YAML header.

```yaml
---
title: "My First Quarto Website Page"
format: html
---
```

Then add Markdown content below the header.

## 2. Learn by example

::: columns
::: column
### Example content

```markdown
# Welcome to Quarto

This page is part of a website built with Quarto.

- Create pages with `.qmd`
- Add navigation in `_quarto.yml`
- Use beautiful themes
```
:::

::: column
### Preview this page

> In the browser, this becomes a nicely formatted website page with headings, lists, and styling.
:::
:::

## 3. Interactive sections

### Tabs example

:::: tabs
::: tab "Why Quarto?"
Quarto lets you build websites, documents, presentations, and books from plain text.
:::

::: tab "Website features"
- Navigation menus
- Table of contents
- Responsive themes
- Code copy buttons
:::
::::

## 4. Add notes and tips

::: callout-tip
Use `code-copy: true` in `_quarto.yml` to let readers copy code examples with one click.
:::

## 5. Create navigation

In `_quarto.yml`, add pages to the navbar:

```yaml
website:
  navbar:
    right:
      - href: index.qmd
        text: Home
      - href: tutorial.qmd
        text: Tutorial
      - href: about.qmd
        text: About
```

## 6. Try a short quiz

::: callout-note
Answer: You can render the full site with `quarto render` and view the generated pages in `_site/`.
:::

### Quick check

1. What is the main project file for a Quarto website?  
2. Which YAML key sets the site theme?  
3. How do you render the website from the terminal?

> **Tip:** Save your answers and then compare with the next section.

## 7. Customize the look

You can change the theme and add custom styles.

```yaml
format:
  html:
    theme: cosmo
    css: styles.css
```

Then add custom CSS in `styles.css`.

## 8. Publish your site

Render the full website:

```bash
quarto render
```

Open `_site/index.html` in your browser.

## 9. Keep learning

- Add more pages like `about.qmd`
- Use images and tables
- Try a Quarto blog or book project later

::: callout-success
Nice work! You now have a website-ready Quarto tutorial site that is easy to extend and customize.
:::
